title: Prime
description: The first hour of the day. Consecration of the day's work to God.

sections.title: Opening
sections.rubric: Make the Sign of the Cross and begin:
sections.latinText:
V. Deus, in adiutórium meum inténde.
R. Dómine, ad adiuvándum me festína.
Glória Patri, et Fílio, et Spirítui Sancto. Sicut erat in princípio, et nunc, et semper, et in sǽcula sæculórum. Amen. Allelúia.
sections.englishText:
V. O God, come to my assistance.
R. O Lord, make haste to help me.
Glory be to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Ghost. As it was in the beginning, is now, and ever shall be, world without end. Amen. Alleluia.

sections.title: Hymn: Iam Lucis Orto Sidere
sections.latinText:
Iam lucis orto sídere
Deum precémur súpplices,
Ut in diúrnis áctibus
Nos servet a nocéntibus.

Linguam refrénans témperet,
Ne litis horror ínsonet:
Visum fovéndo cóntegat,
Ne vanitátes háuriat.

Sint pura cordis íntima,
Absístat et vecórdia:
Carnis terat supérbiam
Potus cibíque párcitas.

Ut cum dies abscésserit,
Noctémque sors redúxerit,
Mundi per abstinéntiam
Ipsi canámus glóriam.

Deo Patri sit glória,
Eiúsque soli Fílio,
Cum Spíritu Paráclito,
Nunc et per omne sǽculum. Amen.
sections.englishText:
Now that the daylight fills the sky,
We lift our hearts to God on high,
That He, in all we do or say,
Would keep us free from harm today.

Would guard our hearts and tongues from strife,
From anger's din would hide our life,
From all ill sights would turn our eyes,
Would close our ears to vanities.

Would keep our inmost conscience pure,
Our souls from folly would secure,
Would bid us check the pride of sense
With due and holy abstinence.

So we, when this new day is gone
And night in turn is drawing on,
With conscience by the world unstained,
Shall praise His name for victory gained.

All praise to God the Father be,
All praise, eternal Son, to Thee,
Whom with the Spirit we adore
Forever and forevermore. Amen.

sections.title: Psalms
sections.rubric: The psalms of Prime vary by day of the week. On Sunday: Psalms 117, 118:1-16. On Monday: Psalm 1, 2. The psalms are concluded with the Gloria Patri.
sections.latinText: [Proper psalms — change daily by the day of the week]
sections.englishText: [Proper psalms — change daily by the day of the week]

sections.title: Short Chapter
sections.latinText:
Regi sæculórum immortáli, invisíbili, soli Deo, honor et glória in sǽcula sæculórum. Amen.
R. Deo grátias.
sections.englishText:
Now to the King of ages, immortal, invisible, the only God, be honor and glory for ever and ever. Amen.
R. Thanks be to God.

sections.title: Versicle & Closing
sections.latinText:
V. Dóminus vobíscum.
R. Et cum spíritu tuo.
Orémus.
Dómine Deus omnípotens, qui ad princípium huius diéi nos perveníre fecísti: tua nos hódie salva virtúte; ut in hac die ad nullum declinémus peccátum, sed semper ad tuam iustítiam faciéndam nostra procédant elóquia, dirigántur cogitatiónes et ópera. Per Dóminum nostrum Iesum Christum Fílium tuum, qui tecum vivit et regnat in unitáte Spíritus Sancti, Deus, per ómnia sǽcula sæculórum. R. Amen.
sections.englishText:
V. The Lord be with you.
R. And with thy spirit.
Let us pray.
O Lord God Almighty, who hast brought us to the beginning of this day: defend us in the same by Thy power; that we may not fall into any sin this day, but that all our thoughts, words, and works may be directed to the fulfillment of Thy justice. Through our Lord Jesus Christ Thy Son, who liveth and reigneth with Thee in the unity of the Holy Ghost, God, world without end. R. Amen.
